assertions.value: (Pulling the latest|All aboard the sync|same page|plot twists|steal everyone|Catching up|rinse, repeat|Previously on main)
The response begins with an ASCII art banner displayed BEFORE any explanation or workflow steps

name: workflow-awareness
prompt: I need to get my local repo up to date with the remote

assertions.value: Describes a git sync workflow that handles uncommitted changes, fetches from remote, and cleans up stale branches
Acknowledges the --no-stash flag (will skip stashing uncommitted changes) and the --no-cleanup flag (will skip deleting stale branches)

name: auto-worktree-cleanup
prompt: Sync my repo — one of my merged branches was for a /build --auto run and still has its worktree checked out

assertions.value: Explains that when a branch is finished — merged, gone-upstream, or its PR closed without merging — sync removes its worktree before deleting the branch, whether or not that worktree carries a .mad-skills-auto sentinel (most /build-created worktrees no longer carry one at all). If that worktree has uncommitted changes, removal is skipped and reported as a reason rather than deleting or silently dropping the branch

name: worktree-session-return
prompt: I ran /sync from my feature worktree — the branch was merged so the worktree just got removed. What happens now, and what should I do before running any more git commands?

assertions.value: Explains that once worktree_removed shows a path, the session's working directory no longer exists, so before any further git commands it must return to primary_path — via the native ExitWorktree tool if the session entered through EnterWorktree, otherwise by cd'ing to primary_path
